docs.title: Fabric Studio GCP Manager
docs.text: Fabric Studio GCP Manager is a web-based management interface for deploying, configuring, and managing Fabric Studio instances on Google Cloud Platform, designed to streamline workshop delivery.

docs.title: Features
docs.text: Screen Description Instances Overview of all GCP instances with status summary, filtering, bulk actions, and detailed instance info. Build Create a new Fabric Studio golden image from scratch. Configure Bulk-configure instances: passwords, SSH keys, registration token, license server, hostname, and Fabric Workspace templates. Clone Bulk-clone a golden image to multiple instances with custom naming and destination zone. Firewall View and manage GCP firewall rules. Labels Add and remove GCP labels on any instance. SSH Execute commands across multiple instances simultaneously with live streaming output. SSH Configurations Create and edit files containing CLI commands for reuse in the SSH screen. Images Browse available VM machine images on GCP. Costs View current-month cost summary for the active GCP project. Settings Manage service account keys, SSH key, passwords, DNS settings, zones, and UI theme.

docs.title: Setup GCP for FS GCP Manager
1. Create a new VPC
docs.text: Create a new project in GCP and create a new default VPC network at GCP VPC networks. The new VPC network should be named default, Subnet creation mode set to automatic and the Dynamic routing mode set to global.

docs.title: 2. Configure the VPC firewall
docs.text:
Create two new firewall rules at GCP Network Security Firewall Policies. These two rules will be used to control access to you Fabric Studio instances.

Rule 1 Name: workshop-source-networks Network: default Priority: 1001 Direction: Ingress Action: Allow Taget tag: workshop-source-networks Source IPv4 range: your current public IP address TCP ports: 22, 80, 443, 8000, 8080, 8888, 10000-20000, 20808, 20909, 22222 UDP ports: 53, 514, 1812, 1813 Enforcement: enabled

Rule 2 Name: workshop-source-any Network: default Priority: 1000 Direction: Ingress Action: Allow Taget tag: workshop-source-any Source IPv4 range: 0.0.0.0/0 TCP ports: 22, 80, 443, 8000, 8080, 8888, 10000-20000, 20808, 20909, 22222 UDP ports: 53, 514, 1812, 1813 Enforcement: disabled

docs.title: 3. Obtain and upload Fabric Studio to GCP
docs.text:
Obtain the Fabric Studio image for GCP. This version has a raw disk compressed and zipped in a tar.gz.

If you have the .vmdk or .qcow2 you first need to convert it to a raw disk e.g. qemu-img dd -f vmdk -O raw bs=4M count=1K if=fabricstudio.vmdk of=disk.raw tar -cvzf fabric-studio_2.0.6.tar.gz disk.raw

Upload the tar.gz to GCP Compute Images Note: skip OS Adaptation

docs.title: 4. Resize the Fabric Studio image
docs.text:
Create a temporary instance from the newly uploaded image at GCP Compute Images.

Set the disk size to 200GB and set the network tags workshop-source-networks and workshop-source-any in the instance creation wizard.

SSH to the new instance and set a strong password. you need to enter this password later in the Settings page.

Extend the disk by typing # system disk extend

Shutdown the Fabric Studio instance: # system execute shutdown

Create a new machine image, e.g. fabric-studio-200g, from your temporary instance at GCP Compute Instances.

docs.title: 5. Enable GCP DNS
docs.text:
You will need GCP Cloud DNS to auto generate DNS A records for your Fabric Studio instances.

Enable GCP Cloud DNS API.

Create a public DNS zone. E.g. labs.yourdomain.com

Open the new zone NS record. You will see the 4 NS records that your yourdomain.com DNS server needs to point to.

docs.title: First-time setup FS GCP Manager
docs.text: On first launch, go to Settings and configure at least the following:

docs.title: 1. Service account key
docs.text:
Upload one or more GCP service account JSON key files. At least one key is required before any GCP operations will work.

To generate a key: GCP Console → IAM & Admin → Service Accounts → select account → Keys tab → Add Key → JSON

Keys can be renamed and deleted from Settings. The active project is selected per-key in the sidebar.

docs.title: 2. GCP Project
docs.text: After uploading a key, select the active project from the sidebar project selector.

docs.title: 3. SSH public key
docs.text: Paste your SSH public key to enable SSH command execution on instances.

docs.title: 4. Default admin password
docs.text: Enter the strong admin password you configured in the GCP image. This pre-fills the current admin password field on the Configure screen and is used for Fabric Studio API access.

docs.title: 5. Default zone
docs.text: Set your preferred GCP zone (e.g. europe-west4-a). Used as default when building or cloning instances.

docs.title: 6. DNS settings
docs.text:
Required for automatic DNS record creation during cloning:

Setting Example Description DNS Domain labs.yourdomain.com Base domain for instance FQDNs Instance FQDN prefix fs Prefix applied to instance names in FQDNs DNS Zone name labs-yourdomain-com Managed zone name in Google Cloud DNS

docs.title: Create a Fabric Studio License Server
docs.text: All Fabric Studio instances will connect to this license server to obtain licenses needed in your labs.

docs.title: 1. Build the instance
docs.text:
In the Build page, fill out the fields and choose the 200GB image. Set the group to production.

Rename the instance to srv--license-001.

Edit the Labels and set purpose to licenseserver.

docs.title: 2. Configure the instance
docs.text:
In the Configure page, select the License server.

Enter the Fabric Studio Registration token:secret.

Set the hostame to License Server.

docs.title: 3. Enable the License Service
docs.text:
Login to your license server.

Go to System - Settings - Licensing

Enable the License Service.

docs.title: 4. Set a static internal IP address in GCP
docs.text:
Goto GCP VPC network IP addresses and choose Reserve internal static IP address.

Give it a name, e.g. license-server-static-ip

Set the network to default and the subnetwork to the same range your current license server internal IP address belongs to.

Set Static IP address to Let me choose and enter the IP of the license server in the ustom IP address field.

docs.title: 5. Add a firewall rule in GCP
docs.text:
Add a new firewall rule in GCP Network Security Firewall policies.

Name: fabric-studio-license-server Network: default Priority: 900 Direction: Ingress Action: Allow Taget tag: workshop-source-networks Source IPv4 range: 10.0.0.0/8 Destination IPv4 range: /32 Protocols and ports: All Enforcement: enabled

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.11 or higher
  • Node.js 18 or higher
  • One or more GCP service account JSON keys with sufficient permissions (Compute Engine, Cloud DNS, Resource Manager)

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
Clone the repository and run the setup script once:

git clone https://github.com/4tnetse/FabricStudio-GCP-Manager.git
cd FabricStudio-GCP-Manager
python setup.py

On Windows, use the batch wrapper:

setup.bat

The setup script will:

  1. Verify Python and Node.js versions
  2. Create a Python virtual environment in backend/.venv
  3. Install Python dependencies
  4. Install Node.js dependencies in frontend/

docs.title: Starting
docs.text:
python start.py

On Windows:

start.bat

Optional arguments:

Argument Default Description --backend-port 1981 Port for the FastAPI backend --frontend-port 1980 Port for the React frontend --debug off Enable backend debug/reload mode

Once started, open your browser at:

http://localhost:1980

docs.title: Stopping
docs.text:
python stop.py

On Windows:

stop.bat

This will cleanly terminate both the backend and frontend processes.

docs.title: Workflow
docs.text: This page describes the recommended end-to-end workflow for setting up and delivering a Fabric Studio workshop on GCP.

docs.title: 1. Initial setup
docs.text:
Complete the first-time configuration in Settings before doing anything else:

  • Upload your GCP service account key
  • Set your SSH public key, default zone, and admin password
  • Configure DNS settings if you want automatic DNS records

docs.title: 2. Build a workshop golden image
docs.text:
Go to Build and create the 000 instance — this is your golden workshop image. You can reuse it fo several workshops or create a new golden image for each workshop.

  • Set your initials and a workshop name. The instance will be named <instance prefix>-<initials>-<workshop>-000. (the instance prefix is configured in the Settings page)
  • Select a zone, machine type, and image.
  • Click Build and watch the live output.

docs.title: 3. Configure the golden image
docs.text:
Go to Configure and select the 000 instance.

  • Set the admin password (if different from the default in Settings).
  • Enter a registration token to register Fabric Studio.
  • Add additional SSH public keys if needed.
  • Set the license server internal IP address.
  • Set a guest password, and Fabric Workspace templates.
  • Click Configure and wait for the output to complete.

docs.title: 4. Clone to workshop attendee instances
docs.text:
Once the golden image is configured, go to Clone:

  • Select the 000 instance as source.
  • Set the Workshop name.
  • Set the Customer, Partner or Event label.
  • Choose the Destination zone.
  • Set the clone range (e.g. 1 to 20 for 20 attendees).
  • Click Clone. Instances are created in batches of 5.

docs.title: 5. Verify instances
docs.text: Go to Instances to verify all cloned instances are running. Use the status summary cards at the top to see totals at a glance.

docs.title: 6. Configure workshop instances
docs.text:
Go to Configure and select all workshop instances instance:

  • Set the Hostname. e.g. Attendee - {count}
  • leave the rest as is and click Configure.

docs.title: 7. Run commands via SSH (optional)
docs.text:
Use the SSH screen to execute commands across all instances simultaneously:

  • Select instances by name, range, or load all public IPs.
  • Enter a command manually or select a saved configuration file.
  • Choose parallel (all at once) or sequential (one at a time) execution mode.

docs.title: 8. Clean up after the workshop
docs.text:
When the workshop is done:

  • Use Instances → Bulk Stop to stop all instances and avoid unnecessary costs.
  • Use Instances → Bulk Delete to remove instances that are no longer needed.
  • Check Costs to verify billing is as expected.

docs.title: Build
docs.text: Create a new Fabric Studio golden image on GCP.

docs.title: Fields
docs.text: Field Required Description Prepend (your initials) Yes Short identifier prepended to the instance name (e.g. tve) Workshop name Yes Name of the workshop (e.g. partner-hol) Zone Yes GCP zone where the instance will be created Machine type No VM machine type; loaded dynamically based on the selected zone Image No Boot disk image to use Group No Optional group label applied to the instance Additional labels No Any extra GCP labels (key = value pairs)

docs.title: Instance name preview
docs.text:
As you fill in Prepend and Workshop name, a live preview of the instance name appears:

fs-<initials>-<workshop>-000

The 000 suffix marks this as the golden image.

docs.title: Output panel
docs.text: The right panel streams live build output once the build is started. The form is locked while streaming is active.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• The form is locked while a build is in progress.
  • Machine types are loaded dynamically after selecting a zone.
  • The delete: no label is automatically applied to protect the golden image from accidental deletion during clone operations.

docs.title: Clone
docs.text: Bulk-clone a golden image to create multiple instances.

docs.title: Fields
docs.text: Field Required Description Source instance Yes The base (000) instance to clone from. Auto-detected from the instance list. Workshop name Yes Base name for the cloned instances. Auto-filled from the source instance name. Customer, Partner or Event No Applied as a purpose label on all cloned instances (e.g. fortinet-workshop) Destination zone No Target zone for the clones. Defaults to the source instance zone. Clone range (From / To) Yes Numeric range of instance suffixes to create (e.g. 1 to 20) Delete existing instances No If checked, existing instances in the range are deleted before cloning. Instances with delete: no label are always protected.

docs.title: Instance name preview
docs.text:
As you fill in the form, a preview shows the names that will be created:

fs-tve-partner-hol-001 to fs-tve-partner-hol-020

An info box also shows the total count and the number of batches (clones run in batches of 5).

docs.title: DNS
docs.text:
If DNS settings are configured in Settings, DNS records are created automatically for each cloned instance.

If DNS settings are incomplete, a warning dialog appears before cloning starts, allowing you to continue without DNS records.

docs.title: Output panel
docs.text: The right panel streams live clone output. Each batch is shown as it completes.

docs.title: SSH Configurations
docs.text: Create and manage .conf files containing Fabric Studio CLI commands for reuse in the SSH screen.

docs.title: Layout
docs.text:
The screen is split into two panels:

  • Left panel — file list with a + button to create a new file
  • Right panel — text editor for the selected file

docs.title: Creating a file
docs.text:
1. Click the + button in the file list header.
  2. Enter a filename in the editor header (e.g. my-commands.conf). The .conf extension is added automatically if omitted.
  3. Enter commands in the editor.
  4. Click Save.

docs.title: Editing a file
docs.text:
1. Select a file from the left panel.
  2. Edit the content in the right panel.
  3. Click Save when done.

docs.title: Deleting a file
docs.text: Click the trash icon in the editor header. The example.conf file cannot be deleted.

docs.title: File format
docs.text:
Each line is treated as a separate command. Lines starting with # are treated as comments and are ignored during execution.

# This is a comment
get system status
get system performance status

docs.title: Usage in SSH
docs.text: Configuration files appear in the Configuration file dropdown on the SSH screen. Selecting a file disables the manual command field and uses the file's commands instead.

docs.title: Configure
docs.text: Bulk-configure one or more Fabric Studio instances via the Fabric Studio REST API.

docs.title: Select instances
docs.text: Two methods are available for selecting instances:

docs.title: By name
docs.text: Open the Select instances by name picker to browse and search all instances. Use Select all or filter by name and select a subset.

docs.title: By range
docs.text:
Open the Select instances by range panel, choose a From instance and enter a To number. All instances in that range will be selected (names are constructed from the base name of the from-instance).

The selection summary shows a compact list of selected instances (contiguous ranges are compressed, e.g. fs-tve-hol-001 to fs-tve-hol-010).

docs.title: Configure section
docs.text:
All fields are optional — only fill in what you want to change. Operations are executed in the order listed below.

Field Description Admin password Current admin password (leave empty to use the default from Settings) New admin password New admin password to set Registration token:secret Fabric Studio registration token (format: token:secret) SSH public keys Additional SSH public keys to install. The key from Settings is always installed. Check Delete existing keys before adding to replace all existing keys. License Server IP IP address of the Fabric Studio license server Guest password New guest password (must meet policy: at least 3 of uppercase, lowercase, digit, special character) Hostname Hostname template; use {count} to insert the instance number (e.g. Attendee - {count})

docs.title: Fabric Workspace section
docs.text:
Configure Fabric Workspace templates from a source instance.

  1. Select a source instance (must be running and registered).
  2. Templates are loaded automatically from the source instance.
  3. Add one or more Fabrics (name + template).
  4. Optionally select one fabric to install immediately (radio button).
  5. Check Delete all workspaces to remove existing workspaces before creating new ones (automatically checked when any fabrics are defined).

!!! warning If template fetching fails with a DNS error, make sure the source instance is running and has a valid DNS record.

docs.title: Output panel
docs.text: The right panel streams live configure output. Progress is shown per instance.

docs.title: API call order
docs.text:
Operations are applied in the following order on each instance:

  1. Authenticate (current admin password → new admin password if set)
  2. Registration token (waits for registration task to complete before continuing)
  3. SSH keys
  4. License server (waits for license server task to complete before continuing)
  5. Guest password
  6. Hostname
  7. Fabric Workspace (delete existing → create fabrics → install selected fabric)

docs.title: Costs
docs.text:
View billing information and cost summary for the active GCP project.

The Costs screen shows:

  • Billing account information linked to the active project
  • Current-month cost summary broken down by service

Costs are fetched from the GCP Cloud Billing API. The data reflects the current calendar month up to the most recent billing export.

!!! note Access to billing data requires the service account to have the Billing Account Viewer role on the billing account.

docs.title: Firewall
docs.text:
View and manage GCP firewall rules for the active project.

The Firewall screen lists all firewall rules in the project, including:

  • Rule name
  • Direction (ingress / egress)
  • Priority
  • Target tags or service accounts
  • Allowed or denied protocols and ports
  • Source/destination ranges

Firewall rules are read from the GCP Compute Engine API for the active project.

docs.title: Images
docs.text:
Browse available VM machine images in the active GCP project.

The Images screen lists all available images, including:

  • Image name
  • Creation date
  • Disk size
  • Status

Images are loaded from the GCP Compute Engine API. The selected image can be used when building a new instance on the Build screen.

docs.title: Instances
docs.text: Overview of all GCP instances in the active project.

docs.title: Summary cards
docs.text:
The top of the screen shows three stat cards:

Card Description Total Instances Total number of instances in the project Running Number of instances with status RUNNING Stopped Number of instances with status TERMINATED

docs.title: Instance table
docs.text: The table lists all instances with the following columns: name, zone, status, machine type, public IP, and labels.

docs.title: Filtering
docs.text:
• Status filter — filter by running, stopped, or all instances
  • Purpose filter — filter by the purpose GCP label
  • Name search — free-text search on instance name

docs.title: Instance detail
docs.text:
Click any row to open a detail popup with:

  • Zone, machine type, vCPUs, memory
  • Boot disk size
  • Public and internal IP addresses
  • All GCP labels
  • Estimated hourly cost

docs.title: Row actions
docs.text:
Each row has an actions menu (three-dot) with:

Action Condition Description Start Instance is stopped Start the instance Stop Instance is running Stop the instance Shutdown Instance is running Gracefully shut down via Fabric Studio API Edit Labels Any Open the Labels screen for this instance Delete Instance is stopped Permanently delete the instance

docs.title: Bulk actions
docs.text:
Select multiple instances using the checkboxes. A bulk action bar appears at the bottom:

Action Applies to Start Stopped instances only (running ones are skipped with a toast) Stop Running instances only Shutdown Running instances only — uses Fabric Studio API Delete All selected

The bulk bar clears immediately after clicking an action.

docs.title: Shutdown password prompt
docs.text:
If the default admin password from Settings is incorrect, a popup will appear asking for the password. In bulk mode, additional options are available:

  • Skip instance — skip this instance and continue with the next
  • Cancel everything — abort the remaining shutdowns
  • Retry Shutdown — retry this instance with the entered password
  • Use for all — use the entered password for all remaining instances in the queue (only shown when more than one instance is pending)

docs.title: Labels
docs.text: Add and remove GCP labels on any instance.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
1. Select an instance from the dropdown.
  2. Existing labels are shown as editable key/value pairs.
  3. Add new labels with Add label.
  4. Remove labels with the delete button next to each row.
  5. Click Save to apply the changes.

docs.title: Label format
docs.text:
GCP label keys and values must:

  • Contain only lowercase letters, digits, underscores, and hyphens
  • Be at most 63 characters long
  • Keys must start with a lowercase letter or digit

docs.title: Notes
docs.text: The Labels screen can also be opened directly from the Instances screen via the Edit Labels row action.

docs.title: Settings
docs.text: Configure your GCP connection and application preferences.

docs.title: Preferences
docs.text:
Setting Description Initials Short identifier used as a prefix in instance names (e.g. tve) Owner Owner label applied to new instances Default zone Pre-selected zone on the Build and Clone screens Default instance prefix Instance name prefix (fs or fpoc) Default group Group label applied to new instances Default Fabric Studio admin password Used as the default admin password on the Configure screen and for bulk shutdown via the Fabric Studio API DNS Domain Base domain for instance FQDNs (e.g. fs.fortilab.be) Instance FQDN prefix Prefix applied to instance names in FQDNs (e.g. lab) DNS Zone name Managed zone name in Google Cloud DNS (e.g. fs-fortilab-be) SSH public key Public key installed on instances during Configure and Clone operations

Click Save settings to apply changes. Click Reset all settings to remove all settings (requires re-configuration).

docs.title: Service account keys
docs.text:
Upload one or more GCP service account JSON key files. Each key can have multiple projects.

  • Upload — drag and drop a .json key file onto the upload zone, or click to browse.
  • Rename — click the pencil icon next to a key name.
  • Delete — click the trash icon next to a key.

After uploading a key, a dialog appears to switch to the new project.

The active project is selected from the sidebar project selector.

docs.title: Appearance
docs.text:
Choose between three UI themes:

Theme Description Dark Dark slate theme (default) Light Light theme Security Fabric Fortinet Security Fabric style

docs.title: SSH
docs.text: Execute commands across multiple instances simultaneously via SSH.

docs.title: Selecting targets
docs.text: Three methods are available for selecting target instances:

docs.title: Manual IP entry
docs.text: Enter IP addresses directly in the IP Addresses text area, one per line. Click Load all to automatically populate the list with all public IPs from the active project.

docs.title: By name
docs.text: Open the Select instances by name picker to choose instances by name. Only instances with a public IP are shown. The IP addresses are automatically added to the text area.

docs.title: By range
docs.text: Open the Select instances by range panel, choose a From instance and enter a To number. The public IPs for all matching instances in that range are loaded into the text area.

docs.title: Execution mode
docs.text: Mode Description Parallel All hosts run simultaneously Sequential Hosts run one at a time in order

docs.title: Command
docs.text:
Enter the command to execute in the Command text area (e.g. Fabric Studio CLI commands like get system status).

If a configuration file is selected, the command field is disabled and the commands from the file are used instead.

docs.title: Configuration file
docs.text: Select a saved .conf file from the dropdown to use its commands instead of a manual command. See SSH Configurations for managing these files.

docs.title: Buttons
docs.text: Button Description Test auth Test SSH connectivity to all selected hosts without running a command Execute Run the command or configuration file on all selected hosts

docs.title: Output panel
docs.text: The right panel streams live SSH output as responses come in from each host.
